assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 35-39 detail all 5 criteria: (1) single license filter via is_in with INNER JOIN, (2) comma-separated filter via split(',') and is_in, (3) invalid license returns 400 via Expression::parse with AppError::BadRequest, (4) pagination integration with filter applied before count() and offset/limit, (5) response shape unchanged with Result<Json<PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>>, AppError>. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show Verdict: PASS with detailed reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files match task specification exactly (2 modified, 1 created); no out-of-scope or unimplemented files'. Lines 23-24: 'PR modifies exactly the files specified in the task: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (modified), modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modified), tests/api/package.rs (created). No out-of-scope files. No unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are report/analysis files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). The outputs contain only verification analysis and verdicts. No code modifications are present in any output file. The report references code via quoted snippets for analysis purposes only.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md ends with 'Overall: PASS' and a summary paragraph. No merge commands, auto-merge actions, or merge instructions are present. The report is structured as a verification report for review, ending with '*This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr v0.10.0.*'

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines across all 3 files'. Lines 29: 'Scanned all added lines across 3 files. No hardcoded passwords, API keys, private keys, environment files, cloud credentials, or database credentials detected.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | 1 new test file (`tests/api/package.rs`) with 4 test functions; no modified or deleted test files'. Line 48: 'Test Change Classification: ADDITIVE -- tests/api/package.rs is a new file with 4 new test functions and associated assertions. No test files were modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all expected rows: Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS), Sensitive Patterns (PASS), CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Test Quality (PASS, combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality N/A per line 13), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE), Verification Commands (N/A). The detailed sections are organized by domain: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 23-25 covering Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability), 'Security' (lines 29 covering Sensitive Patterns), 'Correctness' (lines 33-40 covering CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands), 'Style/Conventions' (lines 44-48 covering Convention Upgrade, Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, Test Change Classification).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 23-25): file-by-file scope listing ('modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (modified), modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modified), tests/api/package.rs (created)'), diff size ('~80 lines of additions across 3 files'), and commit reference. Security (line 29): 'Scanned all added lines across 3 files. No hardcoded passwords, API keys, private keys, environment files, cloud credentials, or database credentials detected. The spdx::Expression import and license string handling do not introduce sensitive data.' Correctness (lines 34-40): per-criterion code-level evidence citing specific code constructs (is_in filter, split(','), Expression::parse, AppError::BadRequest) and corresponding test names. Style/Conventions (lines 44-48): detailed test quality assessment ('4 test functions...each testing distinct behavior', 'They share the TestContext setup but differ in seeding, query parameters, and assertions. Not parameterization candidates.'). Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive code-level evidence for each acceptance criterion.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Eval Quality: N/A'. Line 47: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found on this PR.' Test Quality is PASS (line 13) with Eval Quality listed as N/A, meaning it did not affect the combined verdict.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 47). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the outputs. Root-Cause Investigation is N/A (line 6: 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'). No sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These appear as the first two rows of the summary table (lines 5-6), before the domain-specific verdicts (lines 7-15), indicating they are determined independently from the domain sub-agent analysis.
